description: Build a bulk CSV upload feature for invoice processing. Users should be able to upload a CSV file with up to 10,000 invoice records, with real-time validation feedback and progress tracking. The backend must parse, validate, and queue invoices for async processing.

comments.author: d.park
comments.body: This is our main feature commitment for Sprint 47. Let's make sure we have the CSV validation layer solid before we ship.

comments.author: alex.chen
comments.body: Started the upload endpoint. Parsing logic is done, working on validation rules now.

comments.author: sarah.mitchell
comments.body: Alex — please prioritize this. GlobalTech Corp is piloting this feature and the VP is watching closely.

summary: Real-time payment dashboard with live metrics
description: Build a real-time payment operations dashboard showing transaction volume, success rates, processing times, and error rates. Uses WebSocket for live updates. Must support filtering by time range, payment method, and customer tier.

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: I'll pick this up once my current task is done. Need to sync with Alex on the WebSocket schema.

summary: Webhook notification service for payment events
description: Design and implement a webhook notification service that sends real-time payment event notifications to integrated third-party services. Must support retry with exponential backoff, event signing for security, and a dashboard to monitor delivery status.

comments.author: priya.sharma
comments.body: PR is up for review. @alex.chen can you take a look at the retry logic when you get a chance?

description: Implement role-based access control for multi-tenant architecture. Define roles (admin, operator, viewer, billing) with granular permissions per resource. Must include middleware enforcement in API gateway and database-level row security policies.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: Starting the DB schema design this week. Need to finalize the role hierarchy with David.

summary: Invoice PDF generation with branded templates
description: Create a PDF generation service that renders invoices using customer-branded HTML templates. Supports custom logo, color scheme, and footer text per tenant. Output is streamed directly to S3 for download.

comments.author: priya.sharma
comments.body: Merged to main. PDFs are generating correctly for all test templates.

description: Build a self-service refund portal allowing customers to initiate and track refund requests. Includes approval workflow for amounts over $500, automatic refund processing via Stripe for approved amounts, and email notifications at each step.

summary: Bulk CSV invoice upload returns 500 error for enterprise customers
description: Enterprise customers attempting to upload invoice CSVs > 500 rows receive a 500 Internal Server Error. The error occurs during the validation phase — the system runs out of memory when processing large files synchronously. Applies to all enterprise-tier customers. Logs show 'Allocation failed — JavaScript heap out of memory' on the Node.js side.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: Reproduced locally with a 2000-row CSV. The validation step loads the entire file into memory before processing. We need stream-based parsing.

comments.author: alex.chen
comments.body: Looking into this now. Planning to switch to streaming CSV parser and add a file size limit with proper error messaging.

comments.author: sarah.mitchell
comments.body: Alex — this is now escalated to VP level. GlobalTech Corp is blocked. Must fix by EOD tomorrow (Tuesday). I've emailed the full escalation details. Please treat this as your #1 priority.

summary: Payment retry loop consumes excessive DB connections
description: The payment retry mechanism creates a new DB connection for each retry attempt instead of reusing from the pool. Under peak load with 500+ concurrent failed payments, this exhausts the connection pool and causes cascading failures across all payment processing.

comments.author: priya.sharma
comments.body: Noticed connection pool metrics spiking during the GlobalTech outage yesterday. The retry handler needs a semaphore or circuit breaker.

summary: Invoice due date calculation off by one day for UTC- timezones
description: Customers in UTC-5 to UTC-8 timezones report invoice due dates showing one day earlier than expected. The due date calculation uses UTC midnight but doesn't account for the customer's timezone offset when generating the display date.

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Reported by a customer in PST. Due date shows 01/14 instead of 01/15.

summary: Notification email template broken for HTML clients
description: Payment confirmation emails sent to enterprise customers using HTML email clients (Outlook, Gmail) render broken tables. The responsive template uses unsupported CSS properties. Fallback to plain text works but looks unprofessional.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: Minor but makes us look bad in client demos. Let's fix when we get a chance.

summary: Dashboard load time > 10s for enterprise accounts with 50k+ invoices
description: Dashboard queries for enterprise accounts with over 50,000 invoices take over 10 seconds to load. The invoice list query does a full table scan instead of using the indexed pagination. Carried over from Sprint 46.

comments.author: d.park
comments.body: Alex — Jordan's PR review is blocking this. Can you prioritize the review so we can unblock Marcus?

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: I've got the fix ready but it depends on Alex's bulk upload changes. Need that PR merged first.

description: Upgrade PostgreSQL from version 13 to 16 across all environments. Must test compatibility of existing schemas, review breaking changes (particularly around query planner and replication), and coordinate zero-downtime migration with the DevOps team.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: Tested locally with a copy of prod data. The new query planner handles our analytics queries 2x faster. Need to schedule the prod cutover.

summary: Set up staging environment auto-deploy pipeline
description: Configure GitHub Actions to auto-deploy the main branch to staging environment after successful test suite. Includes smoke tests, database migration automation, and Slack notification on deployment status.

comments.author: priya.sharma
comments.body: Almost done. Just need to finalize the smoke test script.

summary: Migrate email templates to new templating engine
description: Migrate all transactional email templates from the legacy EJS-based renderer to the new React-email based templating engine. Affects 12 templates: invoice, refund, payment confirmation, password reset, etc. Carried over from Sprint 46.

comments.author: d.park
comments.body: Low priority — we can defer if higher priority items come up.

summary: Write runbook for payment service incident response
description: Document the incident response runbook for the payment service. Cover: common failure modes, on-call escalation paths, database recovery procedures, and post-mortem template. Carried over from Sprint 46.

comments.author: d.park
comments.body: We need this before the next on-call rotation starts. Alex, can you prioritize this?

summary: Implement CSV parsing validation for bulk upload endpoint
description: Implement CSV parsing with row-by-row validation. Must validate column headers, data types, required fields, and provide detailed error reporting for malformed rows. Use streaming parser to avoid memory issues identified in PHOE-1198.

comments.author: alex.chen
comments.body: Switched to streaming CSV parser. Validation rules are implemented for all 12 columns. Need to add the enterprise row limit check.

summary: Add progress tracking for large CSV uploads
description: Add real-time progress indicator for large CSV uploads. Show percentage complete, rows processed, validation errors found, and estimated time remaining. Use WebSocket events from backend to update frontend.

summary: Write integration tests for webhook delivery retry
description: Write integration tests covering webhook delivery with retry scenarios: successful delivery on first attempt, delivery succeeds after retry, delivery fails after all retries exhausted, and concurrent delivery of multiple webhook events.

comments.author: priya.sharma
comments.body: Tests are written and passing locally. Included in the main PR for PHOE-1147.
